data.text: I’m checking the server review-session area and the referenced docs first so I can decide whether this bead is small enough to execute as-is or needs decomposition. After that I’ll wire the schema/persistence contract to the existing server code and verify the exact tests the bead requires.

data.output:
# Bead Authoring Template

> A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent
> sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation.
> — Reliability principle **P7** (`docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`)

This document is the canonical template + rubric for creating beads in
this repository. It exists because the 2026-05-03 bead-quality audit
(`.ddx/executions/20260503T155638-bead57f0cb9e/bead-quality-audit-2026-05-03.md`)
found that only **1 of 20** representative open beads scored 8/8 on
the sufficient-sub-agent-prompt rubric. The most common failure modes
were: no specific test names in AC (80 % miss), no `go test` +
lefthook gate in AC (80 % miss), and no root cause with file:line in
description (65 % miss).

---

## 1. The 8-criterion rubric

Every bead must satisfy:

| # | Criterion | Verifier |
|---|-----------|----------|
| (a) | **Title**: one-line scope clarity, imperative, names subsystem + change | "Pagination off-by-one in bead list endpoint" not "Pagination bug" |
| (b) | **Description**: problem + ROOT CAUSE WITH FILE:LINE + proposed fix + non-scope | Root cause cites `cli/path/file.go:42-50` |
| (c) | **AC**: numbered, verifiable, includes specific test names | At least one AC names `TestFoo` or a `go test -run` filter |
| (d) | **AC** includes "wired-in" assertion when introducing new code paths | New function → AC asserts a caller invokes it (production or test) |
| (e) | **AC** includes `go test` command + lefthook gate | Both `cd cli && go test ./...` and `lefthook run pre-commit` named |
| (f) | **Labels**: phase + area + kind + cross-refs | `phase:N`, `area:*`, `kind:*`, plus adr/spec/prevention if applicable |
| (g) | **Parent + Deps**: explicit | Parent set; deps either listed or "no deps" stated |
| (h) | Description reads as **sufficient sub-agent prompt** | A competent agent given only the bead body can pick a file and run tests without asking |

Score 8/8 = execution-ready. Less = retrofit before dispatch.

---

## 2. Template fields

```text
TITLE
  <area>: <imperative one-line scope>
  e.g. "agent: unify ExecuteLoopSpec across cobra/HTTP/server layers"

TYPE
  task | bug | epic | chore

LABELS
  phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>
  + cross-refs: prevention, adr:<id>, spec:<id>, story:<n>, refactor

PARENT
  <ddx-id> of governing epic / story / spec
  (omit only if this is a root)

DEPS
  - <ddx-id-1>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  - <ddx-id-2>: <one-line why this dep matters>
  (or explicit "No deps.")

SPEC-ID (when applicable)
  --set spec-id=FEAT-NNN  | SD-NNN | TD-NNN | ADR-NNN

DESCRIPTION
  PROBLEM
    <one-paragraph statement of what is broken or missing>

  ROOT CAUSE
    <function/file:line where the defect lives>
    <what the code does today vs what it should do>
    For new features: ROOT CAUSE -> "no existing implementation"
    is OK; replace with CURRENT STATE describing the gap.

  PROPOSED FIX
    <bulleted concrete changes; cite file:line for each>
    - cli/internal/foo/bar.go:120-145  rename Baz -> Quux
    - cli/internal/foo/bar_test.go      add TestQuux_RoundTrip

  NON-SCOPE
    - <thing readers might assume is included but isn't>
    - <thing covered by sister bead ddx-NNNN>

  INTERSECTIONS (optional)
    - <ddx-id>: <how this bead relates to that one>

ACCEPTANCE
  1. <verifiable assertion 1 — names a Test, file path, command, or
     observable artifact>
  2. <verifiable assertion 2>
  3. ...
  N-1. cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green.
  N. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 3. Examples

### 3.1 Example of a strong bead (audit score 8/8)

`ddx-1e516bc9` — *"fizeau Execute: explicit Harness + empty Model + non-empty Profile/MinPower must route within harness's eligible models"*

Why it scored 8/8:

- **Title (a)** names subsystem (`fizeau Execute`), the precondition (`explicit Harness + empty Model`), and the expected behavior (`route within harness's eligible models`).
- **Description (b)** opens with **OBSERVED** (one paragraph, file:line) → **SCOPE — UPSTREAM (fizeau) ONLY** → **INVESTIGATION FIRST** (with explicit close-as-no-op outcome) → **UPSTREAM CHANGE** (file:line into `service_execute.go:193-224`, `models.yaml:31-60`, `registry.go`) → explicit **NOT IN SCOPE** list naming five excluded paths.
- **AC (c)** names two concrete tests:
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelWithProfile_RoutesWithinHarness` and
  `TestExecute_ExplicitHarnessEmptyModelNoProfile_FailsClearly`.
- **Wired-in (d)**: AC #6 asserts the resolved model surfaces in
  `.ddx/workers/<id>/spec.json`, `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/result.json`, and the
  workers UI — three distinct call-graph endpoints.
- **Test command + lefthook (e)**: AC #9 — `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green; lefthook pre-commit passes.`
- **Labels (f)**: `phase:2, area:agent, kind:fix, upstream-fizeau, observed-failure`.
- **Parent + Deps (g)**: parent `ddx-e34994e2`; dep `ddx-cfedee8e` with explanation that the ladder must supply MinPower for fizeau to act on.
- **Sufficient (h)**: a sub-agent given only the bead body knows which file in fizeau to read, what the gap looks like, what the negative-test outcome should be, and how to verify the end-to-end fix without asking.

### 3.2 Example of a weak bead (audit score 1/8) — annotated

`ddx-0793ac75` — *"federation: federated GraphQL resolvers + routing-metadata fields"*

Verbatim description:
```
GraphQL resolvers that aggregate across spokes (federatedWorkers,
federatedRuns, federatedDocuments, etc.). Add routing-metadata fields
exposing which spoke owns each result. Write-routing contract: hub
forwards mutations to owning node, never broadcasts (Story 15
dependency).
```

Verbatim AC:
```
1. federated* resolvers in schema.
2. Routing-metadata visible per result.
3. Mutations route to owner; tested.
4. Tests cover happy + degraded paths.
```

Failures, criterion by criterion:

- **(b) FAIL** — no problem statement (what's broken today?), no root cause, no file:line, no non-scope. The reader cannot tell where the schema lives, where resolvers go, or what "routing-metadata" should look like as a Go type.
- **(c) FAIL** — "tested" and "Tests cover happy + degraded paths" name no `Test*` function. Sub-agent has no way to know what to write or where.
- **(d) PARTIAL** — "tested" implies the resolvers are wired, but no production-call-graph assertion (e.g., "schema.graphql Mutation block exposes field X").
- **(e) FAIL** — no `go test` command, no lefthook gate.
- **(h) FAIL** — a sub-agent reading this cold cannot answer: which schema file? which resolver package? what fields on routing-metadata? what's the wire format? Hand-curation by the operator is the only path forward.

**Retrofit shape** (illustrative — not an actual edit):
```
PROBLEM
  Story 14 needs aggregate-across-spokes resolvers. Today the GraphQL
  schema (cli/internal/server/graphql/schema.graphql) exposes only
  per-node Worker/Run/Document queries. Federation requires
  federated* counterparts that fan out to spokes and stitch results.

ROOT CAUSE
  No federated resolver layer exists. cli/internal/server/graphql_resolver.go
  contains only single-node resolvers. Spoke fan-out is in
  cli/internal/server/federation_spoke.go but nothing in the GraphQL
  layer calls it.

PROPOSED FIX
  - schema.graphql: add Federated{Worker,Run,Document} types with
    `ownerSpoke: String!` field.
  - graphql_resolver.go: add federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
    federatedDocuments resolvers calling federation_spoke.FanOut.
  - graphql_mutation.go: write-routing helper routes mutations to
    owning node via federation_spoke.RouteToOwner.

NON-SCOPE
  - New non-federated resolvers (out: covered by Story 13).
  - Spoke discovery (out: covered by ddx-fe63969f).

ACCEPTANCE
  1. schema.graphql exposes federatedWorkers/federatedRuns/
     federatedDocuments queries with ownerSpoke metadata.
  2. TestFederatedWorkers_FanOutAggregates verifies fan-out + stitch.
  3. TestFederatedMutation_RoutesToOwner verifies write-routing.
  4. TestFederatedWorkers_DegradedSpoke returns partial results +
     warning.
  5. cd cli && go test ./internal/server/... green.
  6. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
```

---

## 4. Authoring checklist (paste into your editor before running `ddx bead create`)

```text
[ ] (a) Title: imperative, one line, names subsystem + change
[ ] (b) Description has all four sub-elements:
        [ ] PROBLEM (one paragraph)
        [ ] ROOT CAUSE with file:line citation (or CURRENT STATE for new features)
        [ ] PROPOSED FIX (bulleted, file:line per item)
        [ ] NON-SCOPE
[ ] (c) Each AC line is verifiable; at least one names Test* or `go test -run`
[ ] (d) New code path → AC asserts a production or test caller invokes it
[ ] (e) AC includes:
        [ ] cd cli && go test ./<paths>/... green
        [ ] lefthook run pre-commit passes
[ ] (f) Labels: phase:<N>, area:<subsystem>, kind:<category>, +cross-refs
[ ] (g) Parent set; deps listed or "no deps" stated
[ ] (h) Self-test: re-read the bead from cold. Could a competent
        sub-agent execute it without asking a question? If no, retrofit.
[ ] No /tmp/ paths cited as load-bearing context. Inline the relevant
    excerpt instead — /tmp does not survive between machines.
```

---

## 5. Anti-patterns surfaced by the audit

- **`/tmp/` references as load-bearing context.** 5 of 20 audited beads cite a `/tmp/...` plan file. These are functionally broken links to a sub-agent on a different machine. **Inline the excerpt.**
- **"See parent" without inline summary.** Refactor children of `ddx-5cb6e6cd` (C4/C7/C11/C13) systematically defer detail to the parent epic. Each child fails the stand-alone test. **Inline the parent's relevant 5-10 lines.**
- **Vague test phrases.** "Tests cover X." "All execute-bead tests still pass." Both omit the actual `Test*` name or `-run` filter the executor needs.
- **Story-doc copy-paste.** One-paragraph beads (`ddx-0793ac75`, `ddx-0e5c3005`, `ddx-1d52a30c`, `ddx-0ca4ffda`) are story excerpts that were never expanded for execution. Story documents are *upstream* of beads, not the bead itself.
- **Implicit lefthook.** Lefthook runs in pre-commit hooks anyway, but if AC doesn't name it, executors omit it from their own verification before committing.

---

## 6. When to skip a criterion


---
# Reliability Principles

See this document for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.

These principles describe how the execution machinery should behave when a layer rejects a candidate, encounters transient failure, or needs to surface degraded state to operators.

## P1: Fail-Open At Every Machinery Layer

When a layer's specific check rejects a candidate, the layer skips itself and emits a structured event. It does not wedge the pipeline.

The auto-routing fix (`workers.go:803`, commit `3b4f5d58`) is the canonical example: preflight is now advisory when no operator pin exists.

## P2: Single Responsibility Per Layer

Each layer rejects only on conditions it owns. Routing pre-flight does NOT decide provider availability (`fizeau` owns); cooldown does NOT decide eligibility (`picker` owns); etc.

Cross-layer concerns are the smell.

## P3: Observable Degradation

Every fail-open emits a structured event surfaced in the workers panel. Operators see "preflight skipped (no operator pin)" instead of silent acceptance OR endless retry loop.

## P4: Bounded Blast Radius

A failure on bead X must not affect bead Y. The stay-alive fix at commit `41cb762e` established this for preflight rejections (per-bead continue, not loop exit). Extend it to all layers.

## P5: Operator-Visible State

Worker reports current state (`idle`, `claiming`, `executing`, `reviewing`, `blocked-on-X`) at all times. No "8 hours running, 0 attempts" mystery state.

ADR-022 rev 5 `§Probe + freshness state model` defines the worker side; the UI workers panel surfaces it.

## P6: Auto-Retry Only For Transient Classes

Cooldown fires ONLY when the model genuinely couldn't make progress (clean no-changes with rationale). Disrupted, preflight-rejected, network-error, claim-race -> no cooldown, return to ready.

Existing code: `shouldSuppressNoProgress` at `execute_bead_loop.go:1545` already respects `Disrupted` (commit `47d8054e`).

## P7: Bead = Prompt

A bead's description + AC must be sufficient context for a competent sub-agent to execute it without hand-curation. Investigation done, file:line citations included, concrete test names specified, explicit non-scope marked.

If a sub-agent succeeds where the bead's auto-prompt failed, the BEAD failed (not the executor). Bead-authoring template enforces this; bead-quality audit (forthcoming bead) retrofits existing beads.

Result\\n    RunAgent-->>User: Result (stdout + session log)\\n```\\n\\n## Layer 1 — CLI flag parse\\n\\n`ddx work` is a thin alias for `ddx agent execute-loop`. All flags are cloned\\nfrom the inner command.\\n\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:10` — `newWorkCommand` constructs the alias.\\n- `cli/cmd/work.go:40` — flags are cloned verbatim from `newAgentExecuteLoopCommand`.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1319` — `--harness` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1320` — `--model` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1321` — `--provider` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1322` — `--model-ref` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1327` — `--local` flag declaration.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1338` — `runAgentExecuteLoop` entry point reads flags.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1342` through `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1357` — flag values\\n  are plucked into local variables (`harness`, `model`, `provider`, `modelRef`,\\n  `effort`, `local`).\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1374` — `--local` branch takes the inline path; without\\n  it the loop submits to the running DDx server.\\n- `cli/cmd/agent_cmd.go:1384` — `ValidateForExecuteLoop(harness, model, provider, modelRef)`\\n  runs before any bead is claimed.\\n\\nThe flag values eventually reach `ExecuteBead`, which in turn builds\\n`RunOptions`:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:62` — `RunOptions` struct definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead.go:498` — `runner.Run(RunOptions{...})`\\n  constructs the options, including `Provider: opts.Provider` on line 504.\\n\\n**Observation:** `RouteFlags` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:51`) has a `Provider` field; so does\\n`RunOptions` (`cli/internal/agent/types.go:74`). They are the raw CLI inputs. The gap is not here\\n— it is at the normalization boundary (Layer 2).\\n\\n## Layer 2 — Normalize flags into `RouteRequest`\\n\\nThe normalization boundary converts raw CLI flags into the minimal routing ask\\nthat the routing engine evaluates.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:289` — `NormalizeRouteRequest(flags RouteFlags, cfg Config, catalog *Catalog) RouteRequest`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:294` — `RouteRequest{Effort, Permissions}`\\n  initial struct. Note the absence of `Provider`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:303` — `flags.Harness != \\\"\\\"` branch assigns\\n  `HarnessOverride` directly.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305` — `flags.Provider != \\\"\\\"` branch: the\\n  provider flag is **only** consulted as a harness-alias lookup. If\\n  `--provider vidar-omlx` does not match any entry in `builtinHarnesses`, the\\n  value is silently discarded.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:318` — `flags.Model != \\\"\\\"` branch routes the\\n  model through `catalog.NormalizeModelRef` to produce either `ModelRef` (known\\n  in catalog) or `ModelPin` (exact, bypasses catalog policy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/types.go:337` — `RouteRequest` struct definition. Fields:\\n  `Profile`, `ModelRef`, `ModelPin`, `Effort`, `Permissions`, `HarnessOverride`.\\n  **No `Provider` field — see Gap #1.**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\n`--provider vidar-omlx` does not name a registered harness, so the provider\\nflag is dropped at `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:305`. `--model-ref qwen/qwen3.6` flows through\\n`NormalizeModelRef`; since it is not in the shared catalog, it becomes a\\n`ModelPin`. The resulting `RouteRequest` carries only\\n`{ModelPin: \\\"qwen/qwen3.6\\\"}`.\\n\\n## Layer 3 — Discovery probe\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:117` — `init()` sets the process-wide cache\\n  TTL to `30 * time.Second`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:124` — `DiscoverProviderModels(workDir, timeout)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:125` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:130` — cache hit returns the cached result if still fresh.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:141` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:155` — probes every configured provider via `providerstatus.Probe`; silently skips unreachable ones (partial failure is tolerated).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:162` — cache write under mutex.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:170` — `InvalidateDiscoveryCache` is the only\\n  way to force a re-probe in tests.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked on the routing hot-path only when a `ModelPin` is not in\\nthe catalog:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:576` — in `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans`, the\\n  discovery probe fires when `req.ModelPin != \\\"\\\" && req.ModelRef == \\\"\\\" && r.Discovery == nil`.\\n\\nDiscovery is invoked a second time on the dispatch path:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` — in `resolveNativeAgentProvider`,\\n  `r.Discovery.ProvidersForModel(model)` looks up which providers advertised\\n  the requested model.\\n\\n## Layer 4 — Candidate planning\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:27` — `BuildCandidatePlans(req, stateOverride)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:33` through `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:46` — iterates every registered harness. `TestOnly` harnesses (script, virtual) are skipped unless `req.HarnessOverride` names them explicitly — this is the guard that prevents `script` from leaking into standard-tier fallback chains (see ddx-869848ec in the comment on line 40).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:47` — calls `evaluateCandidate` per harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:106` — `evaluateCandidate(name, harness, req, stateOverride)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:396` — `rejectIfNotViable(plan, req)` runs the\\n  viability gate. Checks:\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:399` — `!s.Installed`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:402` — `!s.Reachable`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:405` — `!s.Authenticated`.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:408` — quota exhausted.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:411` — degraded.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:414` — policy violation.\\n  - `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:418` — harness override mismatch.\\n\\nNote: none of these check *capability* — context window, tool support, effort\\nlevel support. That is Gap #6.\\n\\n## Layer 5 — Fuzzy matching\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:66` — `FuzzyMatchModel(input)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:70` — delegates to `fuzzyMatchInPool` using\\n  `d.AllModels()` (a flat deduplicated pool across all providers — not per-provider).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:76` — `fuzzyMatchInPool(input, pool)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:78` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:82` — exact-match short-circuit.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:89` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:96` — prefix match: candidate `m` is considered if `len(m) > len(input) && m[:len(input)] == input`. **The comparison is case-sensitive and treats slashes literally (Gap #3).**\\n- `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:100` through `cli/internal/agent/discovery.go:106` — tiebreak: shortest suffix wins; alphabetical on equal suffix length. **Not version-aware (Gap #5).**\\n\\n**Concrete outcome for our invocation:**\\nPool likely contains `Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit`. Input `qwen/qwen3.6` fails the\\nexact check, then fails the prefix check because `Q != q` and the slash is not\\nin the pool entries. The function returns `(\\\"\\\", false)`. `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\\nfalls through to the `isOpenRouterModel` heuristic at\\n`cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:544` (vendor/model format → openrouter),\\nwhich is not what the operator wanted.\\n\\n## Layer 6 — Scoring\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:477` — `scoreCandidate(profile, plan)` entry.\\n  Returns a `float64`; higher is better.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:478` — base score `100`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:485` — `switch profile` over `cheap`, `standard`, `smart`, default.\\n  - `cheap` (lines 486–493): `local` +40, within-quota subscription +20, penalize `cr * 30`.\\n  - `standard` (lines 495–502): `local` +25, within-quota +15, penalize `cr * 10`.\\n  - `smart` (lines 504–512): `cr * 20` bonus (expensive > cheap), within-quota +5.\\n  - default (lines 514–522): treated as standard.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:525` — quota >80%: penalty scales with over-usage.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:528` — quota state unknown: −3.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:531` — routing-signal freshness: `cached` −1, `stale` −4.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:542` — historical success rate (≥3 samples):\\n  ≥0.8 +20, <0.5 −30.\\n\\n**No provider-affinity bonus (Gap #4).** If two harnesses both report viable\\nfor the same requested model, `scoreCandidate` does not look at\\n`plan.Provider` vs the provider the model was discovered on.\\n\\n## Layer 7 — Candidate ranking and selection\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:434` — `RankCandidates(profile, plans)`.\\n  - Assigns scores (line 444) or `-1` for non-viable (line 441).\\n  - Stable sort (lines 448–466): viable before non-viable; descending score;\\n    tiebreak prefers local-cost candidates, then alphabetical harness name.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:556` — `SelectBestCandidate(plans)` returns\\n  the first viable plan, or `\\\"no viable harness candidate: all harnesses rejected\\\"`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/routing.go:567` — `ProbeAndBuildCandidatePlans` is the\\n  full-probe wrapper used by actual dispatch (it calls live `ProbeHarnessState`\\n  per harness before building plans).\\n\\n## Layer 8 — Dispatch\\n\\n`Runner.Run` is the dispatch fork:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:141` — `Run(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:148` — `resolveHarness(opts)` picks the harness.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:376` — `resolveHarness(opts)` definition.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:382` — **Gap #2 origin**: if `opts.Harness == \\\"\\\"` and\\n  `opts.Provider != \\\"\\\"`, the `--provider` value is looked up as a harness\\n  alias. If it matches a registered harness (e.g. `agent`, `local`), that\\n  harness is chosen; otherwise `resolveHarness` falls back to `Config.Harness`.\\n  This is a parallel path that bypasses `NormalizeRouteRequest` entirely.\\n\\nDispatch branches (in order):\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:165` — `harnessName == \\\"virtual\\\"` → `RunVirtual(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:170` — `harnessName == \\\"agent\\\"` → `RunAgent(opts)` (embedded).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:175` — `harnessName == \\\"script\\\"` → `RunScript(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:187` — HTTP-provider harnesses with no binary (`lmstudio`, `openrouter`): surfaces the harness name as `opts.Provider` and delegates to `RunAgent`. The comment on lines 185–186 references `ddx-501e87ef` — the bug where cheap-tier attempts were burning <1s with \\\"exec: no command\\\".\\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:278` — default exec path: `Executor.ExecuteInDir(ctx, harness.Binary, args, stdin, execDir)`.\\n\\nEmbedded agent dispatch:\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:65` — `RunAgent(opts)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:94` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider(wd, model, opts.Provider, opts.ModelRef)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:464` — `resolveEmbeddedAgentProvider` entry.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:493` — `resolveNativeAgentProvider(workDir, model, explicitProvider, explicitModelRef)`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:519` — **Gap #2 again**: `explicitProvider != \\\"\\\"` overrides the default provider with the raw `opts.Provider` string. This is where `--provider vidar-omlx` finally re-enters the flow, but by now the routing engine has already ranked candidates without knowing about it.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:521` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:548` — discovery-based provider resolution: if the model isn't in the catalog and discovery has results, route to the first provider that advertises the model (exact or fuzzy).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:555` — `cfg.BuildProviderWithOverrides(providerName, overrides)` materializes the provider object from the native agent config.\\n\\n## Layer 9 — Wire-level HTTP request\\n\\nThe embedded agent library builds the request body and dispatches to the\\nprovider endpoint. DDx hands off at `agentlib.Run`.\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:127` — system prompt construction via\\n  `prompt.NewFromPreset(agentCfg.Preset).WithTools(tools).WithContextFiles(...).WithWorkDir(wd).Build()`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:153` — `agentlib.Request` assembly:\\n  `Prompt`, `SystemPrompt`, `Provider`, `Tools`, `MaxIterations`, `WorkDir`,\\n  `Metadata`, `Compactor`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — `agentResult, err := agentlib.Run(ctx, req)`\\n  — handoff into the `github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/agent` library. DDx does not\\n  own the HTTP transport from here on.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:688` — `buildAgentProvider(cfg)` creates\\n  the provider used by the request. Branches:\\n  - `openai-compat` or `openai` (line 691): `oai.New(oai.Config{BaseURL, APIKey, Model})`.\\n  - `anthropic` (line 697): `anthropic.New(anthropic.Config{APIKey, Model})`.\\n  - `virtual` (line 702): `virtual.New(virtual.Config{DictDir})`.\\n\\nFor `vidar-omlx`, the native-config resolution at `resolveNativeAgentProvider`\\nloads the provider block from `~/.config/agent/config.yaml` or\\n`.agent/config.yaml`. The HTTP layer is OpenAI-compatible; the request body is\\nthe standard `/v1/chat/completions` payload built inside the agent library,\\nwith tool schemas appended.\\n\\nThe agent library exposes its wire-level dump via the `AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE`\\nenvironment variable (introduced in ddx-agent v0.3.12 — see ddx-a0f7a4bb).\\n\\n## Layer 10 — Response parsing\\n\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:320` — same line; `agentlib.Run` returns\\n  `(AgentResult, error)` and DDx inspects the result starting at line 323.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:336` through `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:349` — maps\\n  `agentResult.Tokens`, `agentResult.ToolCalls`, `agentResult.SelectedProvider`,\\n  etc. into DDx `Result`.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:355` — wall-clock deadline → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:358` — idle timeout → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:361` — compaction-stuck circuit breaker →\\n  error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:364` — stall detector (30 consecutive\\n  read-only tool calls) → error.\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:367` — generic agent error, including the\\n  `ErrProviderRequestTimeout` wrapper (line 369).\\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner.go:380` — agent non-success status (e.g.\\n  `StatusCancelled`).\\n\\nThe `unexpected end of JSON input` error reported in ddx-6a5dfe35 surfaces\\nfrom inside the agent library's streaming decoder; DDx sees it via the generic\\nerror branch at line 367 and forwards the message unchanged. The agent library\\nowns the decode (DDx does not duplicate JSON parsing at this layer).\\n\\n## Cross-References\\n\\n- [SD-015 Agent Routing and Catalog Resolution](SD-015-agent-routing-and-catalog-resolution.md) — intent/specification.\\n- [SD-023 Agent Routing Visibility](SD-023-agent-routing-visibility.md) — routing evidence and\\n  `ddx agent doctor` projections.\\n- [FEAT-006 Agent Service](../../01-frame/features/FEAT-006-agent-service.md) — the enclosing feature.\\n\\n## Maintenance — CI citation check\\n\\nThis document's value depends on its citations matching reality. Fixtures are authored either by hand\\n+// (the checked-in happy/error paths) or regenerated by\\n+// scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh against a live omlx endpoint.\\n+type omlxFixture struct {\\n+\\tMeta   omlxFixtureMeta\\n+\\tFrames []string // raw SSE bodies, written verbatim in order\\n+}\\n+\\n+type omlxFixtureMeta struct {\\n+\\tName        string              `json:\\\"name\\\"`\\n+\\tDescription string              `json:\\\"description\\\"`\\n+\\tExpected    omlxFixtureExpected `json:\\\"expected\\\"`\\n+}\\n+\\n+type omlxFixtureExpected struct {\\n+\\t// Status is \\\"success\\\" or \\\"error\\\".\\n+\\tStatus string `json:\\\"status\\\"`\\n+\\t// ErrorMustNotContain is a substring that the stream error — if any — must\\n+\\t// not contain. The first\\n+// line is the {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\", ...} record; remaining {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":...}\\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\\n+\\tt.Helper()\\n+\\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"open fixture %s\\\", path)\\n+\\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\\n+\\n+\\tvar fix omlxFixture\\n+\\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\\n+\\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\\n+\\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\\n+\\tfirst := true\\n+\\tfor scanner.Scan() {\\n+\\t\\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\\n+\\t\\tif line == \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tvar record struct {\\n+\\t\\t\\tKind string          `json:\\\"kind\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tBody string          `json:\\\"body\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\\\"-\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"parsing fixture line in %s: %v\\\", path, err)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tswitch record.Kind {\\n+\\t\\tcase \\\"meta\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\tif !first {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: meta record must be the first non-empty line\\\", path)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &fix.Meta); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"parsing meta in %s: %v\\\", path, err)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tcase \\\"frame\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\tfix.Frames = append(fix.Frames, record.Body)\\n+\\t\\tdefault:\\n+\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: unknown record kind %q\\\", path, record.Kind)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tfirst = false\\n+\\t}\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, scanner.Err(), \\\"scanning fixture %s\\\", path)\\n+\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Meta.Name, \\\"fixture %s: missing meta.name\\\", path)\\n+\\trequire.NotEmpty(t, fix.Frames, \\\"fixture %s: no frame records\\\", path)\\n+\\treturn fix\\n+}\\n+\\n+// newOMLXReplayServer stands up an httptest.Server that behaves like a vidar-omlx\\n+// /v1/chat/completions endpoint by replaying the fixture's frames verbatim,\\n+// with a short inter-frame flush so the consumer's SSE decoder sees per-chunk\\n+// arrivals rather than one batched blob.\\n+func newOMLXReplayServer(t *testing.T, frames []string) *httptest.Server {\\n+\\tt.Helper()\\n+\\tsrv := httptest.NewServer(http.HandlerFunc(func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {\\n+\\t\\t// Match omlx's advertised Content-Type exactly — the SSE comment-frame\\n+\\t\\t// middleware in provider/openai activates only when Content-Type starts\\n+\\t\\t// with text/event-stream.\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Content-Type\\\", \\\"text/event-stream; charset=utf-8\\\")\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Cache-Control\\\", \\\"no-cache\\\")\\n+\\t\\tw.Header().Set(\\\"Transfer-Encoding\\\", \\\"chunked\\\")\\n+\\t\\tflusher, _ := w.(http.Flusher)\\n+\\t\\tfor _, body := range frames {\\n+\\t\\t\\tif _, err := io.WriteString(w, body); err != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\treturn\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tif flusher != nil {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tflusher.Flush()\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\t// Small delay to reproduce per-frame arrival; long enough that the\\n+\\t\\t\\t// decoder cannot batch frames, short enough that the test stays\\n+\\t\\t\\t// under a second total.\\n+\\t\\t\\ttime.Sleep(5 * time.Millisecond)\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t}))\\n+\\tt.Cleanup(srv.Close)\\n+\\treturn srv\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_ConsumerEndToEnd drives a full ddx work orchestration\\n+// path (Runner.RunAgent → agentlib.Run → openai.Provider.ChatStream) against an\\n+// httptest double that replays recorded omlx SSE wire traces. This is the\\n+// consumer-side smoke test for ddx-agent v0.3.14 — see bead ddx-bbb2d177.\\n+//\\n+// What this guards against:\\n+//\\n+//  1. A future ddx-agent bump reintroducing the SSE comment-frame bug (bead\\n+//     agent-f237e07b): the happy-path fixture includes `: keep-alive\\\\n\\\\n`\\n+//     frames between data frames and must NOT surface \\\"unexpected end of\\n+//     JSON input\\\".\\n+//  2. A DDx-side change breaking the omlx dispatch path (e.g., a header or\\n+//     request-shape change that trips omlx's parser).\\n+//  3. A silent-success regression where the stream is consumed without error\\n+//     but the assembled content is empty or malformed — the happy-path assertion\\n+//     pins both the final content and finish_reason.\\n+//  4. failures are reported via result.ExitCode / result.Error\\\")\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\t// The regression signature is always disallowed — on BOTH paths.\\n+\\t\\t\\t// v0.3.13 would surface this even for well-formed streams when\\n+\\t\\t\\t// keep-alive comment frames were present.\\n+\\t\\t\\tif fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorMustNotContain,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"consumer surfaced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature — the sseCommentFilter in ddx-agent v0.3.14+ is missing or broken\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\n+\\t\\t\\tswitch fix.Meta.Expected.Status {\\n+\\t\\t\\tcase \\\"success\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \\\"happy-path fixture must close cleanly; got error=%q\\\", result.Error)\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \\\"happy-path must produce no error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tcase \\\"error\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \\\"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \\\"error-path must surface a non-empty error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\\\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t})\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. The full replay test\\n+// above would also fail in that case, but a dedicated assertion surfaces the\\n+// structural problem without the noise of a streaming-test stack trace.\\n+func TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist(t *testing.T) {\\n+\\tfor _, name := range []string{\\\"happy_path.jsonl\\\", \\\"error_path.jsonl\\\"} {\\n+\\t\\tp := filepath.Join(\\\"testdata\\\", \\\"omlx-wire\\\", name)\\n+\\t\\tinfo, err := os.Stat(p)\\n+\\t\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"missing fixture %s\\\", p)\\n+\\t\\tassert.False(t, info.IsDir(), \\\"%s must be a file\\\", p)\\n+\\t\\tassert.NotZero(t, info.Size(), \\\"%s must not be empty\\\", p)\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..fd43c76e\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/omlx_smoke_test.go\\n@@ -0,0 +1,66 @@\\n+//go:build omlx_smoke\\n+\\n+// Opt-in live smoke test for the ddx work → ddx-agent → vidar-omlx consumer\\n+// path. Unlike the fixture-replay test in omlx_e2e_test.go (which ships in CI\\n+// and uses a recorded test double), this test hits a real omlx endpoint and is\\n+// intended for manual release verification against a live server. It is gated\\n+// by the `omlx_smoke` build tag so the default `go test ./...` never runs it.\\n+//\\n+// Run:\\n+//\\n+//\\tOMLX_SMOKE_URL=http://vidar:1235/v1\\\\\\n+//\\tOMLX_SMOKE_MODEL=Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\\\\n+//\\tgo test -tags omlx_smoke -run TestOMLXLiveSmoke -v ./cli/internal/agent\\n+//\\n+// set\\\")\\n+\\t}\\n+\\n+\\tprovider := openai.New(openai.Config{\\n+\\t\\tBaseURL: baseURL,\\n+\\t\\tAPIKey:  os.Getenv(\\\"OMLX_SMOKE_API_KEY\\\"),\\n+\\t\\tModel:   model,\\n+\\t\\tFlavor:  \\\"omlx\\\",\\n+\\t})\\n+\\n+\\tr := NewRunner(Config{SessionLogDir: t.TempDir(), TimeoutMS: 120000})\\n+\\tr.LookPath = mockLookPath\\n+\\tr.AgentProvider = provider\\n+\\n+\\tresult, err := r.RunAgent(RunOptions{\\n+\\t\\tHarness: \\\"agent\\\",\\n+\\t\\tPrompt:  \\\"Reply with the single word 'ready' and nothing else.\\\",\\n+\\t\\tWorkDir: t.TempDir(),\\n+\\t})\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err)\\n+\\n+\\t// The regression signature is the precise error v0.3.13 surfaced when\\n+\\t// omlx emitted keep-alive SSE comment frames during reasoning warmup.\\n+\\t// Any bump that reintroduces it must be caught here before it ships.\\n+\\tassert.NotContains(t, result.Error, \\\"unexpected end of JSON input\\\",\\n+\\t\\t\\\"live omlx produced the v0.3.13 SSE comment-frame regression signature\\\")\\n+\\tassert.Equal(t, 0, result.ExitCode, \\\"live omlx smoke must close cleanly; error=%q\\\", result.Error)\\n+\\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Output, \\\"live omlx must return some /dev/null\\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/README.md\\n@@ -0,0 +1,61 @@\\n+# omlx SSE Wire Fixtures\\n+\\n+These fixtures drive `cli/internal/agent/omlx_e2e_test.go` — the consumer-side\\n+end-to-end smoke test that guards `ddx work` against regressions on the\\n+`ddx-agent → vidar-omlx` dispatch path. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\\n+\\n+## File format\\n+\\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\\n+\\n+- First line: `{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\\n+- Remaining lines: `{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\"<raw SSE body>\\\"}` — one frame per\\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\\\n\\\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\\n+  response in arrival order.\\n+\\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \\\"unexpected end of JSON input\\\" regression) and normal\\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\\n+\\n+## When to regenerate\\n+\\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. Do NOT regenerate\\n+just to refresh timestamps.\\n+\\n+## How to regenerate\\n+\\n+Use `scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh`, which wraps `ddx agent run` with\\n+`AGENT_DEBUG_WIRE_STREAM_FULL=1` (shipped in ddx-agent v0.3.14) so the full\\n+stream is captured to a sidecar JSONL, then reshapes it into the fixture\\n+format:\\n+\\n+```bash\\n+scripts/capture-omlx-fixture.sh \\\\\\n+  --endpoint http://vidar:1235/v1 \\\\\\n+  --model    Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit \\\\\\n+  --prompt   \\\"Reply with the word ready.\\\" \\\\\\n+  --name     happy-path \\\\\\n+  --status   success \\\\\\n+  --out      cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\\n+```\\n+\\n+After the script writes the fixture, hand-edit the `meta.expected` block to\\n+pin the `content_contains` / `error_contains` / `finish_reason` assertions for\\n+the capture you just took — the script cannot infer those from the wire\\n+bytes alone.\\n+\\n+## Why fixtures live here instead of the upstream agent repo\\n+\\n+The agent repo (`DocumentDrivenDX/agent`) has its own provider-level SSE\\n+tests in `provider/openai/openai_test.go`. Those guard the SDK's handling of\\n+SSE framing in isolation. The fixtures in *this* directory guard the DDx\\n+consumer — `ddx work` → `Runner.RunAgent` → `agentlib.Run` — against both\\n+SDK regressions (a bad ddx-agent bump) and DDx-side regressions (a header or\\n+request-shape change that trips omlx's parser). The consumer must surface the JSON parse error cleanly (NOT as 'unexpected end of JSON input', which is the v0.3.13 SSE-comment-frame regression signature). The orchestrator should observe a non-success result and record task_failed without corrupting the bead tracker.\\\",\\\"expected\\\":{\\\"status\\\":\\\"error\\\",\\\"error_must_not_contain\\\":\\\"unexpected end of JSON input\\\",\\\"error_contains\\\":\\\"invalid character\\\"}}\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\": keep-alive\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\"data: {\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"chatcmpl-2\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"choices\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\"index\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"delta\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"role\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"assistant\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"finish_reason\\\\\\\":null}]}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\": keep-alive\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\"data: {\\\\\\\"id\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"chatcmpl-2\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"model\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"Qwen3.6-35B-A3B-4bit\\\\\\\",\\\\\\\"choices\\\\\\\":[{\\\\\\\"index\\\\\\\":0,\\\\\\\"delta\\\\\\\":{\\\\\\\"reasoning_content\\\\\\\":\\\\\\\"thinking...\\\\\\\"},\\\\\\\"finish_reason\\\\\\\":null}]}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\"data: {this is not valid json}\\\\n\\\\n\\\"}\\ndiff --git a/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\\nnew file mode 100644\\nindex 00000000..9eb45cbd\\n--- /dev/null\\n+++ b/cli/internal/agent/testdata/omlx-wire/happy_path.jsonl\\n@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@\\n+{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\",\\\"name\\\":\\\"happy-path\\\",\\\"description\\\":\\\"omlx emits keep-alive SSE comment frames during Qwen3 reasoning warmup, then a role delta, reasoning_content chunks, a content delta, and a final finish_reason=stop chunk followed by [DONE]. do\\n+  case \\\"$1\\\" in\\n+    --endpoint) endpoint=\\\"$2\\\"; shift 2 ;;\\n+    --model)    model=\\\"$2\\\"; shift 2 ;;\\n+    --prompt)   prompt=\\\"$2\\\"; shift 2 ;;\\n+    --out)      out=\\\"$2\\\"; The first\\n+// line is the {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\", ...} record; remaining {\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":...}\\n+// lines are the SSE bodies to stream in order.\\n+func loadOMLXFixture(t *testing.T, path string) omlxFixture {\\n+\\tt.Helper()\\n+\\tf, err := os.Open(path) // #nosec G304 -- test fixtures are checked in.\\n+\\trequire.NoError(t, err, \\\"open fixture %s\\\", path)\\n+\\tt.Cleanup(func() { _ = f.Close() })\\n+\\n+\\tvar fix omlxFixture\\n+\\tscanner := bufio.NewScanner(f)\\n+\\t// Default 64KB scanner buffer is tight for long reasoning lines.\\n+\\tscanner.Buffer(make([]byte, 64*1024), 1024*1024)\\n+\\tfirst := true\\n+\\tfor scanner.Scan() {\\n+\\t\\tline := strings.TrimSpace(scanner.Text())\\n+\\t\\tif line == \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\tcontinue\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tvar record struct {\\n+\\t\\t\\tKind string          `json:\\\"kind\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tBody string          `json:\\\"body\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t\\tRaw  json.RawMessage `json:\\\"-\\\"`\\n+\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\tif err := json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &record); got error=%q\\\", result.Error)\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Empty(t, result.Error, \\\"happy-path must produce no error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ContentContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Output, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"happy-path must assemble content from post-keep-alive data frames\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tcase \\\"error\\\":\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Equal(t, 1, result.ExitCode, \\\"error-path fixture must report failure via ExitCode=1\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.NotEmpty(t, result.Error, \\\"error-path must surface a non-empty error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tif want := fix.Meta.Expected.ErrorContains; want != \\\"\\\" {\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\tassert.Contains(t, result.Error, want,\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"error-path must propagate the underlying parse error\\\")\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t\\tdefault:\\n+\\t\\t\\t\\tt.Fatalf(\\\"fixture %s: unknown expected status %q\\\", name, fix.Meta.Expected.Status)\\n+\\t\\t\\t}\\n+\\t\\t})\\n+\\t}\\n+}\\n+\\n+// TestOMLXFixtureReplay_FixturesExist is a cheap guardrail that fails fast if\\n+// the testdata/omlx-wire directory is emptied or renamed. Context: bead **ddx-bbb2d177**, which\\n+was filed after v0.3.14 shipped the `sseCommentFilter` fix for bead\\n+**agent-f237e07b** (upstream).\\n+\\n+## File format\\n+\\n+Each fixture is a JSONL file with two record kinds:\\n+\\n+- First line: `{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"meta\\\", ...}` — describes the capture and the assertion\\n+  shape the replay test will enforce (expected status, substrings that must or\\n+  must not appear in the assembled content / error).\\n+- Remaining lines: `{\\\"kind\\\":\\\"frame\\\",\\\"body\\\":\\\"<raw SSE body>\\\"}` — one frame per\\n+  record, written verbatim (including `\\\\n\\\\n` terminators) to the test HTTP\\n+  response in arrival order.\\n+\\n+Frames include both `: keep-alive` SSE comment frames (the exact shape that\\n+triggered the v0.3.13 \\\"unexpected end of JSON input\\\" regression) and normal\\n+`data: {...}` chunks.\\n+\\n+## When to regenerate\\n+\\n+Regenerate fixtures only when real omlx server behavior changes in a way the\\n+existing captures no longer represent — for example, a new reasoning-phase\\n+frame shape, a header shift, or a chunk-boundary change. .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:120:{"acceptance":"`ddx agent execute-bead` resolves governing references for both doc-ID and repo-relative path-style `spec-id` values when the target file exists in the execution snapshot; the generated prompt/manifest include the resolved path reference instead of the `No governing references` fallback; and deterministic tests cover a bead whose `spec-id` is a repo-relative path such as `workflows/README.md`.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"cee85e6565224b30b15f689fa7daac8ad5e4a12b","created_at":"2026-04-11T01:18:51.632271275Z","description":"\u003ccontext-digest\u003e\nAlpha validation run from /Users/erik/Projects/helix using `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` against a live HELIX bead whose `spec-id` is the repo-relative path `workflows/README.md`. Evidence includes `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`, `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/manifest.json`, and `cli/cmd/agent_execute_bead.go`.\n\u003c/context-digest\u003e\n\n`ddx agent execute-bead` prompt synthesis failed to resolve a live path-style `spec-id` into governing references, so the generated execution prompt told the agent there were no governing references even though the referenced file exists in the execution snapshot.\n\n## Reproduction\n1. In `/Users/erik/Projects/helix`, inspect bead `helix-fef22846`; it carries `spec-id=workflows/README.md`.\n2. Run `ddx agent execute-loop --once --json` or `ddx agent execute-bead helix-fef22846 --json`.\n3. Open `.ddx/executions/20260411T011535-bbda19d7/prompt.md`.\n4. Observe the prompt includes `- spec-id: workflows/README.md` but the `## Governing References` section says `No governing references were resolved from the current execution snapshot.`\n\n## Observed gap\n`executeBeadResolveGoverningRefs()` builds the doc graph and looks up only by graph document ID. Path-like `spec-id` values that point to real files in the repo are dropped instead of being resolved as governing references.\n\n## Why this matters\nThis is exactly the context the execution prompt tells the agent to read before changing code or docs. When DDx drops an existing repo-relative path reference, agent execution becomes less grounded and the artifact bundle misstates the available governing context.","events_attachment":"ddx-1f434484/events.jsonl","id":"ddx-1f434484","issue_type":"bug","labels":["helix","phase:build","area:agent","area:docs","area:cli"],"priority":1,"spec-id":"FEAT-006","status":"closed","title":"Resolve path-style spec-id values in execute-bead governing references","updated_at":"2026-04-11T05:12:11.237799965Z"}

.ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:161:{"acceptance":"**User story:** As an operator, I expect the Sessions page to reflect reality — if I ran `ddx work` today, today's sessions appear within seconds. And I expect the feed not to silently break again the next time the agent execution path is refactored.\n\n**Acceptance criteria:**\n\n1. **Session-index source of truth.** A single writer (invoked from `service.Execute` or a thin wrapper that every production agent-execution path calls) appends one line per completed agent invocation to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`. Fields are pointer-only: id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath (relative), nativeLogRef (relative). No prompt or response bodies embedded.\n\n2. **Single-call-site guarantee.** Unit test enumerates every production code path that executes an agent (execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, replay if still present) and asserts each appends exactly one index row. If a new path is added later without wiring this up, the test fails.\n\n3. **Visibility restored.** GraphQL `sessions` query reads `sessions.index.jsonl`. Running `ddx work` (or any agent-execution path) against a fixture project causes the new session to appear in a GraphQL query within 2 seconds of completion. E2E test demonstrates this.\n\n4. **Body lazy-load.** A new resolver `session(id: ID!) { prompt response stderr }` loads the heavy fields on demand from the execution bundle (`.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/`) or the agent-log path, without reading every session in the index.\n\n5. **Legacy migration.** A one-shot command (e.g. `ddx agent log reindex`) seeds the index from the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (397 MB corpus on the current repo), preserving historical sessions. Integration test uses a fixture `sessions.jsonl` and asserts the index has one row per legacy entry with all pointer fields populated.\n\n6. **Deprecated-file behavior.** After migration, the legacy `sessions.jsonl` is not read by the server. `ddx agent usage` and related CLI commands (`cli/cmd/agent_usage.go:197,246,348`, `cli/cmd/status.go:179`) are updated to read from the index too, verified by their existing tests.\n\n7. **Perf sanity.** The GraphQL `sessions` list resolves in ≤100ms p95 on a fixture with 10,000 index entries; current behavior times out or OOMs. Benchmark captured in bead notes as baseline + after-fix.\n\n8. **UI note.** If there is a gap in the index (e.g. the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap on the current repo, assuming backfill from executions/agent-logs is not feasible), the Sessions page surfaces a single-line banner \"No sessions recorded between X and Y\" so the gap is not silently invisible. If backfill is feasible, do it instead and skip the banner.\n\n9. **Playwright e2e.**\n   - Seeds a fixture project with `.ddx/executions/` bundles from both before and after a simulated gap.\n   - Runs the migration command.\n   - Opens `/sessions`, asserts the most recent session timestamp matches the latest bundle, asserts a clicked row lazy-loads prompt/response from the bundle rather than from an embedded payload.\n   - Asserts the feed updates live (within 2s) after a new simulated session is written.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"d733f7f20828708c5179f0ceff9462eaa34654eb","created_at":"2026-04-22T20:22:41.990684692Z","description":"## Observed\n\nThe Sessions page at `/nodes/.../projects/.../sessions` shows nothing newer than **2026-04-19**, three days ago. ddx is definitely still running — `.ddx/executions/` contains bundles from 2026-04-22 (today), `agent-s-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-21, `agent-loop-*.jsonl` from 2026-04-20. The UI simply cannot see them.\n\n## Root cause\n\nThe Sessions GraphQL resolver reads exclusively from `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` (`cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go:504-516`). That file stopped being appended on 2026-04-19 19:46.\n\nThe only writer is `Runner.logSession` (`cli/internal/agent/runner.go:847-914`). It is called from four sites today:\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/runner.go:229` (Runner.Run — legacy)\n- `cli/internal/agent/agent_runner_service.go:216`\n- `cli/internal/agent/claude_stream.go:516`\n- `cli/internal/agent/virtual.go:206`\n\nProduction execute-bead / execute-loop no longer goes through any of them. The Runner-to-service refactor series (`5b933103`, `280f58e0`, `74eceda9`, `7cc1eb58`, `e05c785e`, `9f79f037`) routed the common path through `service.Execute` directly and did not carry forward the sessions.jsonl append. The last sessions.jsonl write lines up with the day that refactor set became default.\n\n## Secondary issue (same fix should address)\n\n`sessions.jsonl` is 397 MB for only 895 entries because each entry embeds the full `result.Output` (see `SessionEntry.Response` at `runner.go:879`). Growth is unbounded, there is no rotation, and `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` (`state_graphql.go:476-490`) reads the whole file, parses every entry, and sorts in memory on every query. Even if we just restore the writer, the feed is fragile and will become a perf problem again quickly — relevant to `ddx-ad0db8fd` (bead detail was also O(N) scan).\n\n## Proposed direction\n\nDo not simply paper over by re-plumbing `logSession` into `service.Execute`. The right fix is:\n\n1. **Decide the source of truth for \"a session\".** Current candidates on disk:\n   - `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/{manifest.json,prompt.md,result.json}` — already written per execute-bead attempt.\n   - `.ddx/agent-logs/agent-*.jsonl` and `agent-s-*.jsonl` — per-run streams from the agent harness.\n   - Bead evidence events (kind:cost already written per attempt, see `execute_bead.go:492`).\n   A session record should be derivable from a combination of execution bundles + agent-log streams, not from a separately-appended aggregate. The aggregate should be an **index**, not a **copy**.\n\n2. **Introduce a lightweight index.** A thin `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl` containing `{id, projectID, beadID, harness, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, bundlePath}` — pointer fields only, no embedded response bodies. Existing `sessions.jsonl` is deprecated to a read-only legacy file during a grace period.\n\n3. **Write the index from `service.Execute`** (or a wrapper the execute-bead / run / execute-loop paths all share). One place to touch. Unit test guards that every production code path that executes an agent appends exactly one index row.\n\n4. **GraphQL reads the index, streams bodies on demand.** `sessions { edges { node { ... } } }` reads the index. A new `session(id: ID!) { prompt, response, stderr }` lazy-loads the heavy fields from the execution bundle or agent-log file when the user expands a row.\n\n5. **Legacy sessions.jsonl compatibility.** The index can be seeded once from the existing 397 MB file so historical data is not lost, then the old file is not read again. One-shot migration command (`ddx agent log reindex` or similar).\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Replaying or regenerating sessions for the 2026-04-19 → 2026-04-22 gap. If the evidence exists in executions/agent-logs we can backfill the index; if not, accept the gap and cap it with a dated marker in the UI.\n- A full telemetry rework. This bead just restores visibility and fixes the shape.","events_attachment":"ddx-2ceb02fa/events.jsonl","execute-loop-last-detail":"escalation exhausted: unsupported model \"minimax/minimax-m2.7\" for harness \"gemini\"; supported models: gemini-2.5-pro, gemini-2.5-flash, gemini-2.5-flash-lite","execute-loop-last-status":"execution_failed","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"feature":"FEAT-010","id":"ddx-2ceb02fa","issue_type":"bug","labels":["feat-008","feat-010","feat-006","perf","observability"],"notes":"## 2026-04-22 — User-directed amendment: shard the index\n\nPer user feedback: a single `sessions.index.jsonl` just replays the same problem at smaller scale. The aggregate needs to be split.\n\n### Revised storage shape\n\n- Directory: `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/` (per-project, under each registered project's `.ddx`).\n- Inside: **monthly shards** named `sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`.\n- Each shard is pointer-only, same fields as the previously-proposed index (id, projectID, beadID, harness, surface, model, startedAt, endedAt, durationMs, cost, tokens, outcome, exitCode, bundlePath, nativeLogRef).\n- Writer resolves the target shard from `time.Now().UTC()` at append time and creates the shard file lazily on first write of the month.\n- Heavy bodies (prompt, response, stderr) stay where they already live — `.ddx/executions/\u003cts\u003e-\u003chash\u003e/` and agent-log streams — and are lazy-loaded by the `session(id:)` resolver.\n\n### Why monthly, not daily / size-based / SQLite\n\n- **Daily** — produces tens of thousands of files over the project lifetime; ReadDir becomes the cost.\n- **Size-based** — requires coordinating rotation with the append; race-prone and hard to test.\n- **SQLite** — overkill for first pass; revisit if a single monthly shard grows past tens of MB.\n- **Monthly** — ≤12 shards/year, human-readable filenames, date-range queries map trivially to shard selection, bounded shard size in practice.\n\n### Reader behavior\n\n- Default Sessions query loads the **current + previous shard** (covers \"recent activity\" for any current date).\n- Date-range queries map the range to a shard set and read only those.\n- Pagination beyond the default window triggers older-shard loads on demand.\n- No shard-coordination lock needed: each month's file is append-only and only the current-month shard is actively written.\n\n### Legacy migration\n\n- `ddx agent log reindex` reads the existing `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.jsonl` once and splits by each entry's `timestamp` field into the appropriate monthly shards under `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/`.\n- After migration, the legacy file is renamed `sessions.jsonl.legacy` (not deleted) and never read again by the server. Hand-removal is the user's call.\n- Idempotent: re-running the command merges without duplicating (index row dedup by `id`).\n\n### Revised acceptance criteria (supersedes criteria 1, 5, 6, 7 on original description)\n\n1. **Sharded index writer.** Single shared call site (from `service.Execute` or equivalent wrapper) appends one line to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/sessions-YYYY-MM.jsonl`, computing the shard from `time.Now().UTC()`. Test: write across a simulated month boundary and assert two shards exist with the correct split.\n\n5. **Legacy migration shards too.** `ddx agent log reindex` splits `sessions.jsonl` into monthly shards by entry timestamp. Integration test uses a fixture with entries across 3 distinct months and asserts 3 shards are produced with correct contents and no duplicates on re-run.\n\n6. **Server reads the shard directory.** `GetAgentSessionsGraphQL` reads `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions/*.jsonl`, respects any date-range arg, and loads only the shards that intersect the range. Unit test seeds 6 monthly shards and asserts a 2-month query reads exactly 2 files.\n\n7. **Perf on sharded index.** Default Sessions view (current + previous shard) resolves in ≤50ms p95 on a fixture with 12 monthly shards × 10k entries each (120k total). Capture baseline (single-file read of same corpus) vs sharded in bead notes.\n\n(Criteria 2, 3, 4, 8, 9 from the original acceptance list remain as written.)\n\n### Scope clarifier\n\nThis amendment does not change the single-call-site writer guarantee or the lazy-body resolver — those remain the load-bearing ideas. The amendment is specifically about **how the pointer index is physically stored**.\n## 2026-04-23 — Implementation benchmark and evidence\n\nBaseline: `go test ./internal/agent -run '^$' -bench 'Benchmark(ReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow|LegacySessionsJSONLFullScan)$' -benchtime=1x` measured legacy single-file full scan/sort of 120k legacy-shaped rows with 2KB embedded responses at `BenchmarkLegacySessionsJSONLFullScan-12 1 1731705957 ns/op`.\n\nAfter fix: the same command measured the sharded default-window reader at `BenchmarkReadSessionIndexDefaultWindow-12 1 8103661 ns/op` for 12 monthly shards x 10k rows each (120k total), with the default query reading only the current + previous shard window. Environment footer: linux/arm64, Go go1.26.2.\n\nAcceptance evidence: `TestSessionIndexWritesMonthlyShards`, `TestSessionIndexShardFilesSelectsOnlyIntersectingDateRange`, `TestReindexLegacySessionsSplitsShardsAndDedupes`, `TestRunViaServiceWithAppendsOneSessionIndexRow`, `TestProductionAgentExecutionPathsUseIndexedServiceWriter`, `TestGraphQLAgentSessions`, `TestAgentLogReindexMigratesLegacyFile`, existing agent usage/status/processmetrics tests, and `e2e/sessions.spec.ts` cover writer sharding, bounded reads, migration, lazy body loading, deprecated-file read removal, UI gap banner, and live refresh.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing session index writer: no changes to `service.Execute`, shared execution wrappers, or agent execution paths to append `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing GraphQL changes: no update to `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` for index-backed `sessions` or lazy `session(id:)` body loading.\nMissing migration command: no `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent CLI implementation and no fixture-based migration test.\nMissing CLI usage/status updates: no changes to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` or `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing performance validation: no benchmark or fixture proving 10,000 index entries resolve within the required threshold.\nMissing UI gap/banner behavior and Playwright coverage for `/sessions`, lazy loading, migration, and live feed updates.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\n**AC 1–3**: No `sessions.index.jsonl` schema, writer in `service.Execute`, or updated GraphQL resolver in `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go`.\n**AC 4**: No `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt/response/stderr from execution bundles.\n**AC 5**: No `ddx agent log reindex` command or migration logic.\n**AC 6**: `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` and `cli/cmd/status.go` are untouched.\n**AC 7**: No benchmark captured.\n**AC 8–9**: No frontend changes or Playwright tests.\n\nREVIEW:BLOCK\n\nMissing `service.Execute` or shared agent-execution wrapper change to append one pointer-only row to `.ddx/agent-logs/sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing unit test proving execute-bead, execute-loop, `ddx agent run`, quorum, and replay paths append exactly one index row.\nMissing `cli/internal/server/state_graphql.go` update so GraphQL `sessions` reads `sessions.index.jsonl`.\nMissing new GraphQL `session(id: ID!)` resolver for lazy-loading prompt, response, and stderr from execution bundles or native logs.\nMissing `ddx agent log reindex` or equivalent migration command, plus integration test for legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing updates to `cli/cmd/agent_usage.go` and `cli/cmd/status.go` to stop reading legacy `sessions.jsonl`.\nMissing 10,000-entry GraphQL sessions benchmark and recorded baseline/after-fix bead notes.\nMissing Sessions UI gap banner or feasible backfill behavior.\nMissing Playwright e2e covering migration, latest timestamp visibility, lazy body load, and live feed updates.","priority":1,"session_id":"eb-b9f50300","status":"closed","title":"Sessions UI is blind to post-2026-04-19 activity: service.Execute path no longer writes .ddx/beads-archive.jsonl:198:{"acceptance":"1. **Root cause #1 fixed:** `attempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}` is moved to AFTER the pre-claim hook succeeds. Regression test in `execute_bead_loop_test.go` asserts: with a fake `PreClaimHook` that fails on the first call and succeeds thereafter, the loop processes the same bead on the second iteration (not skipped because of stale `attempted` state).\n\n2. **Root cause #2 fixed:** the post-`Execute` outcome-handling block (lines ~510-862) does not exit the loop on transient `Store.*` errors. Each `return result, err` in that block is converted to: log the error, emit a `kind:loop-error` bead event when possible, set the bead to a short cooldown, and `continue` to the next iteration. The only exception is context cancellation (`ctx.Err()`), which still terminates immediately.\n\n3. **Regression tests for AC-2:** for each `Store.*` call in the outcome block (Unclaim, IncrNoChangesCount, adjudicateNoChanges, CloseWithEvidence on each path, Reopen on each path, SetExecutionCooldown on each path, late AppendEvent), a test injects a transient error from a fake store and asserts: (a) the loop continues, (b) a `kind:loop-error` event is recorded with the failing operation name, (c) `result.Failures++` advances.\n\n4. **End-to-end reproduction guard:** an integration test seeds a bead.jsonl with 3 ready beads. The first returns `no_changes` (executor fake), the second returns `success`, the third returns `already_satisfied`. The loop runs to completion (all three processed) without exiting prematurely. With the pre-fix loop, this test fails on the first bead.\n\n5. **Worker-state semantics preserved:** existing tests that assert worker.State transitions (`running` → `exited` / `failed` / `stopped` / `reaped`) still pass. The fix changes loop *advancement*, not terminal-state classification.\n\n6. **Documented behavior:** `docs/agents/execute-loop.md` (or the closest existing doc) gains a \"loop termination\" section listing the only conditions that terminate the loop: context cancellation, `Once == true`, `PollInterval \u003c= 0` AND `nextCandidate` returns no candidate, `RoutePreflight` rejected. Explicitly states that Store errors during outcome handling do NOT terminate the loop.\n\n7. **Operator workaround retired:** the README's \"if `ddx work` exits early, restart it\" guidance (or equivalent) is removed; that workaround was masking this bug.","archived_at":"2026-05-02T04:37:57Z","closing_commit_sha":"1495ba706ffdc0e7bfb4833487ed068ffe732aeb","created_at":"2026-04-29T19:05:38.552407002Z","description":"## Symptom\n\n`ddx work` (no `--once` flag, no `--poll-interval`, default mode) exits after processing exactly one bead instead of draining the queue. Reproduced four times today on `/home/erik/Projects/agent` with `--harness lmstudio` pinned (which sidesteps the unrelated `ddx-c7081f89` server-worker tier-walk issue):\n\n| Run | Outcome on first bead | Worker terminal state | Queue still non-empty? |\n|---|---|---|---|\n| 2 | `already_satisfied` (`agent-4aa3584e` adjudicated satisfied) | `already_satisfied` / `exited` | yes — 2 ready beads remained |\n| 3 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 4 | pre-claim hook rejected (diverged branch) | `exited`, attempts=0 | yes |\n| 5 | `no_changes` from agent (`agent-6c7a4c11` declined as epic; bead left **claimed**) | `exited`, attempts=1 | yes — 5 ready beads remained |\n\nIn all four runs the loop should have continued to the next ready bead. Per `agent/.claude/skills/ddx/reference/work.md`, only `success`/`already_satisfied` close the bead and `no_changes`/`land_conflict`/`*_failed` unclaim and continue. None of these outcomes is documented or expected to be loop-terminal.\n\n## Root cause #1 (verified)\n\n`cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:382`:\n\n```go\nattempted[candidate.ID] = struct{}{}\n\n// Pre-claim hook: fetch origin + verify ancestry before claiming.\n// On error the bead is skipped for this iteration; the loop\n// continues (ctx is not cancelled).\nif runtime.PreClaimHook != nil {\n    if hookErr := runtime.PreClaimHook(ctx); hookErr != nil {\n        // ... log + emit ...\n        continue\n    }\n}\n```\n\nThe bead is added to `attempted` **before** the pre-claim hook runs. On hook failure (e.g. diverged branch), the loop `continue`s but the bead stays in `attempted`. Next iteration, `nextCandidate()` (line 924) skips the still-ready bead because it's in `attempted`, returns `ok=false`, and the loop exits at line 374 because `PollInterval \u003c= 0`. Single-shot exit with `attempts=0`.\n\n**Reproduces:** runs 3 and 4 above.\n\n## Root cause #2 (suspected — needs targeted instrumentation)\n\nRuns 2 and 5 reached `Execute()` and got a real report back. Worker log shows the entry line `▶ \u003cbead\u003e: \u003ctitle\u003e` but no closing `✓ \u003cbead\u003e → \u003cresult\u003e` line (which is written at line 909). That means the loop exited somewhere between line 488 (`Executor.Execute`) and line 909 (the final summary log).\n\nBetween those bounds, **at least 16 distinct `return result, err` points** trigger if any `Store.*` operation returns an error: `Unclaim` (711, 700), `IncrNoChangesCount` (717), `adjudicateNoChanges` (721), `CloseWithEvidence` (520, 615, 694, 739), `Reopen` (635, 669), `SetExecutionCooldown` (749, 791, 803, 835, 844), late `AppendEvent` (862). Any single transient store error during outcome handling kills the worker.\n\nFor run 5 specifically, `agent-6c7a4c11` is now `Status: in_progress` with `Owner: ddx, Machine: sindri`. The bead was **never unclaimed** despite the agent returning `no_changes` — meaning the loop exited on or before line 711 (`w.Store.Unclaim`) failing. Investigation should look at what error `Unclaim` would return when called against a bead the same worker claimed seconds earlier; possible candidates: tracker-write race with concurrent worker, bead.jsonl re-write during a fetch by the pre-claim hook on a later iteration that already happened (no — pre-claim is pre-claim, not post-execute), or a malformed bead row that fails store-side validation.\n\nIndependent of the specific store error: the structural pattern of \"any Store.* error → exit the entire loop\" is the wrong default. A store error on outcome handling should at most park the bead and continue to the next, never terminate the worker.\n\n## Why this is loadbearing\n\nThe loop is the queue-drain primitive. Every observed failure-to-drain in the last 24 hours of operator complaints traces back to this — beads that should have been the worker's second/third/fourth attempt never got picked up because the worker exited after one attempt, and the operator manually restarts the drain (sometimes after a long delay). The visible queue depth is misleading: half the \"ready\" beads are sitting behind a worker that quietly died.\n\n## In-scope files\n\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go` — fixes for both root causes\n- `cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop_test.go` — regression tests for each path\n\n## Out of scope\n\n- Anything in the `agent` SDK (`/home/erik/Projects/agent`). The loop and all its store interactions are owned by ddx.\n- The pre-claim hook's divergence-detection logic itself — that's correct behavior; the bug is how the loop reacts to the hook's signal.\n- Adaptive min-tier / tier-walk machinery (`ddx-c7081f89`, `ddx-aec9d68c`) — independent.\n\n## Anti-goals\n\n- Do **not** swallow store errors silently. They must be surfaced (event + log) but should not kill the worker on transient/per-bead failures.\n- Do **not** convert the loop's Once/PollInterval semantics — they're correct.\n- Do **not** change worker terminal-state semantics (`exited`/`failed`/`stopped`/`reaped`).","events_attachment":"ddx-37cdb43a/events.jsonl","execute-loop-no-changes-count":1,"id":"ddx-37cdb43a","issue_type":"bug","labels":["area:execute-loop","kind:bug","phase:fix"],"notes":"REVIEW:BLOCK\n\nDiff only contains execution evidence (manifest.json + result.json) under .ddx/executions/. No changes to execute_bead_loop.go, tests, or docs are visible, so none of the 7 acceptance criteria can be evaluated as implemented.","priority":0,"session_id":"eb-842ed336","status":"closed","title":"execute-loop: ddx work exits after one bead instead of draining the queue","updated_at":"2026-04-30T00:04:15.728726551Z"}
